title: Documentation incorrect with regards to RHEL 8 kernel version
body:
### Issue Description

Reference question #21962

Documentation should be updated to reflect support for RHEL 8. If you install container tools on RHEL 8.9 and do podman info -f ‘{{index .Storage.GraphStatus “Native Overlay Diff”}} as non-root user it does reflect that it is defaulting to native overlayfs, but the documentation references support for it in a kernel version that seems only relevant for RHEL 9. (See kernels here) https://access.redhat.com/articles/3078

So, I assume that it is trying to do native overlayfs on RHEL 8.9, or the documentation needs to be updated to reflect a RHEL 8.9 Kernel is supported.

It must work in some fashion as it is defaulting to it with podman info on our systems.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. install container-tools and podman-docker on fully dnf updated RHEL 8.9 system. https://access.redhat.com/articles/3078
2. Check podman info -f ‘{{index .Storage.GraphStatus “Native Overlay Diff”}} as non-root user
3. Returns true
4. Documentation here reflects that a return of true indicates native overlayfs is being used, but per documentation this should not work until perhaps RHEL 9 with a kernel greater than the requirement.

I started down this path due to COPY commands being extremely slow, but unfortunately neither option helped. But, for this issue just requesting a documentation update for https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/performance.md, so others don’t go down the rabbit hole I went down.

### Describe the results you received

podman info -f ‘{{index .Storage.GraphStatus “Native Overlay Diff”}} as non-root user, without sudo.

Returns true

### Describe the results you expected

Returns false as RHEL 8 kernel according to documentation is not supported. Installer should default to a supported configuration, or documentation is off on the supported kernel version.

### podman info output

```yaml
true
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

AWS, EC2

### Additional information

Podman Dockerfile COPY commands extremely slow, which lead to this fact finding mission, but might open a separate issue for that.
